title: Hydrodynamics-based gene transduction
guidelines: {"blocks":[{"key":"vnp0n","text":"This highly efficient in vivo gene transduction technique for laboratory mice was performed basically as described previously (Gene Therapy. 1999 Jul; 6(7):1258-1266). Hepatocytes are most effectively transduced by tail vein injection of a large volume of DNA solution in a short time. Practice with the injection technique is necessary!!!","type":"unstyled","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[{"style":"bold","offset":151,"length":4},{"style":"italic","offset":22,"length":7},{"style":"italic","offset":127,"length":12}],"entityRanges":[],"data":{}}],"entityMap":{}}
materials_text: {"blocks":[{"key":"ine7q","text":"Reagents: \r","type":"unstyled","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":{}},{"key":"m78vw","text":"","type":"unstyled","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":{}},{"key":"nmlon","text":"Sterile phosphate buffered saline (PBS), room temperature.\r","type":"unstyled","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":{}},{"key":"zb0io","text":"","type":"unstyled","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":{}},{"key":"yl47v","text":"Expression vector plasmid DNA.","type":"unstyled","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":{}},{"key":"uwo22","text":"","type":"unstyled","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":{}},{"key":"cqbgw","text":"Equipment","type":"unstyled","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[{"style":"bold","offset":0,"length":9}],"entityRanges":[],"data":{}},{"key":"wkqpu","text":"3ml syringe.\r","type":"unstyled","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":{}},{"key":"mti7u","text":"","type":"unstyled","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":{}},{"key":"seqiz","text":"27-gauge needle.\r","type":"unstyled","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":{}},{"key":"kx49w","text":"","type":"unstyled","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":{}},{"key":"hciux","text":"Heatlamp (Technilab Instruments, Pequannock, NJ).\r","type":"unstyled","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":{}},{"key":"t8b1p","text":"","type":"unstyled","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":{}},{"key":"datby","text":"Mouse holder, if necessary (Mouse Tail Illuminator, Braintree Scientific Inc., MA).","type":"unstyled","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":{}}],"entityMap":{}}
steps: Dilute the plasmid DNA with sterile PBS right before injection. The final concentration of the DNA should be calculated from the total amount of DNA/mouse.
Take 2 ml of the solution into a 3 ml syringe and put a 27-gauge needle to the syringe. Avoid any air bubbles in the syringe.
Warm up a mouse using a Heatlamp for 1-2 minutes.
Fix the mouse in a Mouse Tail Illuminator.
Inject the DNA solution from the tail vein in 5-7 seconds and put the mouse back into the cage. Mice may become less active transiently, but recover within 10 minutes.
C/EBPbeta is required for 'emergency' granulopoiesis by Hirai, H. et al. published in Nature Immunology on 18 Apr, 2006 https://dx.doi.org/10.1038/ni1354
creator: Protocols Importer
